commit.message:
Remove wrapper mode, add FAIRVISOR_LLM_FORMAT for LLM proxying (#75)

* Add .deb packaging, OpenResty dependency, and systemd service (clean PR)

* Update README with apt installation and service verification instructions

* Remove wrapper mode, add FAIRVISOR_LLM_FORMAT for LLM proxying (closes #74)

- Delete wrapper.lua, wrapper_access.lua, wrapper_body_filter.lua
- Add FAIRVISOR_LLM_FORMAT env var (openai/anthropic/gemini; unset = plain proxy)
- Add FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_AUTH env var for upstream key injection in access.lua
- Move streaming cutoff logic (replace_streaming_cutoff) to llm_limiter.lua
- Simplify access.lua, body_filter.lua, header_filter.lua, init_worker.lua
- Update nginx.conf.template, entrypoint.sh, env_config.lua
- Update README for new LLM proxy model
- Update/remove wrapper unit tests and feature files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add unit tests for FAIRVISOR_LLM_FORMAT, upstream auth injection, streaming cutoff

- llm_limiter_spec: add replace_streaming_cutoff scenarios (openai/anthropic/gemini/no-marker)
- access_spec: add upstream auth injection scenarios with FAIRVISOR_LLM_FORMAT and FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_AUTH mocking
- env_config_spec: add scenarios for llm_format and upstream_auth field loading
- Restore coverage lost from wrapper_spec.lua deletion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Cover remaining replace_streaming_cutoff branches (no-finish-chunk, nil-format, non-string)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add targeted coverage tests for streaming and kill_switch edge cases

Cover 13 previously-uncovered lines across streaming.lua and kill_switch.lua
to fix the coverage regression introduced when wrapper.lua was removed.

streaming.lua (+6 lines):
- is_streaming returns false for empty body
- is_streaming returns false when headers is not a table
- is_streaming returns true when stream field is boolean true
- validate_config returns true for nil config
- validate_config returns error for non-table config
- validate_config returns error when streaming field is not a table

kill_switch.lua (+7 lines):
- validate rejects non-table kill_switches value
- validate rejects empty scope_key
- validate rejects route that is an empty string
- validate rejects non-string expires_at
- check() fallback parses iso8601 when _expires_epoch not pre-set by validate

Non-generated coverage: 91.47% → 91.75% (gate: ≥ 91.64%)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: replace FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_AUTH with FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_HEADER_* prefix mechanism

Closes #80. Removes the single FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_AUTH variable and
replaces it with a generic FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_HEADER_<NAME> prefix
convention that supports injecting any number of upstream HTTP headers.

- entrypoint.sh packs all FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_HEADER_* env vars into
  FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_HEADERS_PACKED (newline-separated Name=value pairs)
  with _ → - conversion on the header name suffix
- nginx.conf.template declares FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_HEADERS_PACKED instead
  of FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_AUTH
- access.lua parses the packed string and calls ngx.req.set_header for
  each entry; header injection is no longer gated on FAIRVISOR_LLM_FORMAT
- env_config.lua drops the upstream_auth field
- Tests and feature scenarios updated accordingly; README updated with
  the new env var pattern

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: remove stale wrapper mode references from README

- Quick start "Which mode" callout: replace Wrapper bullet with updated
  reverse proxy description mentioning FAIRVISOR_LLM_FORMAT
- Mermaid LLM proxy diagram: replace bare upstream key comment with
  FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_HEADER_* reference

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: restructure Installation section — Docker first, then apt

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add FAIRVISOR_STRIP_REQUEST_HEADERS to drop client headers before proxying

Closes #82. Adds a comma-separated env var that lists request headers to
strip after rate-limit keying but before upstream header injection.

- access.lua: parse FAIRVISOR_STRIP_REQUEST_HEADERS, call
  ngx.req.clear_header for each entry (trim whitespace, skip empty)
- nginx.conf.template: declare env FAIRVISOR_STRIP_REQUEST_HEADERS
- Tests: 4 new scenarios (single header, multi-header, strip+inject
  combo, unset no-op)
- README: document alongside FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_HEADER_*

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: expand README for FAIRVISOR_STRIP_REQUEST_HEADERS

- docker run example now shows STRIP + UPSTREAM_HEADER together
- Replace sparse one-liner with a structured "Header handling" section
  explaining both env var families, their naming convention, and the
  order of operations (key → strip → inject → forward)
- Update LLM proxy mermaid diagram to show step 3: strip+inject

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(auth): normalize auth API user payload

Summary:
- normalize `/api/v1/auth` responses to an `AuthUser` before storing
- switch default mocks and success assertions to the flat user payload
- keep compatibility coverage for nested `{ user: ... }` responses

Rationale:
- the ticket background and existing repo models describe a flat user JSON,
  while the earlier frontend implementation assumed a nested payload
- normalizing both shapes removes the reviewer ambiguity without depending on
  one undocumented response wrapper

Tests:
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run test:run -- src/features/auth/__tests__/use-auth-mutation.test.ts
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run test:run
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run build
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run lint

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(frontend): add auth API mutation after Firebase login

Summary:
- add TanStack Query client setup and a shared API client for
  authenticated POST /api/v1/auth requests
- implement auth types, mutation hook, store fields, and login page
  wiring to fetch and persist backend user data after Google sign-in
- cover the auth flow with MSW-backed hook tests and login/store
  updates

Rationale:
- the frontend needs to exchange the Firebase idToken for backend user
  data immediately after login so downstream features can rely on the
  Go auth response
- keeping backend auth user data in Zustand separates Firebase session
  state from app-specific profile data and avoids repeating API logic

Tests:
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run lint
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run test:run
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(frontend): wrap login click handler for lint

Summary:
- wrap the login button click callback with `void` so the handler no
  longer passes a Promise-returning function directly to JSX
- keep the existing Firebase popup flow and UI behavior unchanged

Rationale:
- GitHub Actions runs ESLint in the frontend CI job and rejected the
  initial implementation on `@typescript-eslint/no-misused-promises`
- this keeps the feature behavior intact while making the branch CI
  clean

Tests:
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run lint
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run test:run -- src/pages/__tests__/login-page.test.tsx
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run test:run
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(frontend): add Google login page

Summary:
- add a shadcn card-based login page with a Google sign-in action,
  loading state, and popup-cancel-safe error handling
- switch App to render the login page while keeping the auth store
  listener mounted
- cover the new page behavior with dedicated Vitest cases and align
  the App test with the new entry screen

Rationale:
- SHA-20 requires the frontend to reach Firebase popup login before
  the later auth API and routing tickets land
- the placeholder home screen and missing Card component prevented
  the requested login UI from existing in the app shell

Tests:
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run test:run -- src/pages/__tests__/login-page.test.tsx
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run test:run
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Add starvation hints and negotiation retry for collision fairness

DeepSeek lost all 16 collisions in a session due to passive insistence
choices and an unretried HTTP 500. Fix with two mechanisms:

- Starvation hints in reaction/negotiation prompts when an agent loses
  ≥2 consecutive collisions, informing them so they can self-adjust
- generateWithRetry for Tier 2/3 API calls (max 2 retries, linear
  backoff, abort-aware, cancellation-safe)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(checkout): pass variant data through all checkout flows (#1022)

Variant data (variantId, variantAttributes) was silently dropped during
checkout, causing "cannot call jsonb_each_text on a non-object" for
products with variants. The bug existed in web checkout, the API route,
and the mobile storefront.

- Zod schema: accept both camelCase and snake_case variant_attributes
- API route: read both key conventions, default to {} instead of null
- Web checkout: forward variantId/variantAttributes from cart to order
- Web cart hook: block add-to-cart when has_variants && !variantId
- Mobile: add variant_attributes to CartItem, build from selections,
  forward through checkout and order service, redirect ProductCard
  quick-add to detail page for variant products

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
⚡ Bolt: Add sizes prop to Next.js Image fill to improve LCP (#1024)

* perf(web): add sizes prop to Next.js image fill component in builder config

When using `fill` on a Next.js Image component without specifying `sizes`,
Next.js defaults to `100vw`. This forces the browser to download the
largest image size possible, significantly harming Largest Contentful Paint (LCP)
and overall performance on smaller viewports.

This change adds an appropriate responsive `sizes` prop.

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add sizes prop to file-uploader Image preview (CodeRabbit)

Grid uses cols-2/sm:cols-3/md:cols-4 so sizes reflect 50vw/33vw/25vw.

commit.message:
Structured LLM output, four-tier collision negotiation, CollisionResolvedEvent

Reaction output is now structured JSON ({ speech, insistence }) instead of
free-form text, with fallback parsing for backward compatibility. Collision
resolution replaces binary insist/yield with a four-tier system: pre-declared
insistence comparison (zero API calls) → multi-round three-level negotiation
(max 3 rounds) → bystander voting → random tiebreak, guaranteeing convergence.

New CollisionResolvedEvent domain event carries full resolution metadata
(tier, negotiation rounds, votes with raw text), enabling the history
projector to render tier-aware and perspective-specific descriptions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
refactor: 重大重构 - 纠正对 OpenAI Harness Engineering 的误读

基于 Codex 深度评估，发现我们严重误读了 OpenAI 原文：

核心误读：
- 把"零手写代码"误读成"零人工干预"
- 把"team of agents"误读成"固定七角色流水线"
- 把"repo-specific"误读成"通用框架"
- 过度外推 OpenAI 内部数据

OpenAI 原文真正说的：
- "Humans steer. Agents execute."
- "Humans always remain in the loop"
- 人类负责：目标、优先级、验收、架构决策
- Agent 负责：写代码、测试、PR、响应 review
- 强依赖特定仓库，不能泛化

主要变更：
- README 完全重写，回归原文精神
- 去除所有"零干预""全自动"误导性表述
- 移除固定七角色流水线设计
- 承认当前实现是"概念文档+演示脚本"

下一步：
需要重新实现真正的 harness engineering：
- 单主循环（不是多角色）
- 结构化知识系统
- 可执行环境
- 机械化约束
- Execution plan 一等公民
- 持续 garbage collection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>
